extension.valueMarkdown:
She was enrolled in a Phase III clinical trial (NCT05820295 — a real-ish trial ID) comparing pembrolizumab + carboplatin/pemetrexed vs standard chemo alone for first-line treatment of stage III NSCLC. She signed informed consent in December 2024 and began treatment in January 2025. She has completed 4 cycles of chemo-immunotherapy with partial response on interim imaging. She continues maintenance pembrolizumab with regular monitoring.

The research institute (MD Anderson) uses a Permission Ticket to access Patricia's historical medical records from her community PCP and pulmonologist, covering the 3 years prior to enrollment through the present, to correlate treatment response with prior health status.

Three sites, one state (TX). Tests Permission Ticket constraints on periods (research needs 2022–present, not her full medical history) and scopes (research needs Condition, Observation, DiagnosticReport, MedicationRequest, Procedure — broad but defined by study protocol).

Suspicious 3-cm opacity in the right upper lobe with irregular borders. No pleural effusion. No bony abnormalities identified. Cardiac silhouette normal.

note.text: New diagnosis based on lipid panel: LDL 132, total cholesterol 218, triglycerides 160. Patient declined statin therapy, prefers continued dietary modification.
Well-controlled on lisinopril 10 mg daily; BP 132/80 at goal.
BMI 28.0. Patient is physically active with daily walking. Encouraged continued exercise and dietary adherence.
HbA1c 6.0%, stable in prediabetic range. Continued dietary management, goal HbA1c <5.7%.
LDL 138 mg/dL, total cholesterol 224, triglycerides 170. 10-year ASCVD risk ~12%. Statin therapy discussed; patient elected dietary modification trial with 6-month lipid recheck.

Bilateral knee pain worsening over 2-3 months, mechanical pattern. Exam: mild crepitus bilaterally, no effusion, full extension, flexion to 120 degrees, stable ligaments, non-antalgic gait. Consistent with early degenerative osteoarthritis. Started naproxen 250 mg BID PRN. Counseled on weight management and low-impact exercise.

8 weeks duration, unresponsive to benzonatate 2-week trial. In context of unintentional weight loss, declining hemoglobin, mildly low albumin, and new right upper lobe opacity on chest X-ray, lung malignancy must be excluded. Referred to pulmonology.

Approximately 8 lbs lost over past 2 months (164 lbs in March 2024 to 156 lbs on 08/01/2024). Significant and warrants workup. In context of persistent cough, declining hemoglobin, and new right upper lobe opacity, concerning for malignancy.
Hemoglobin 11.8 g/dL, declined from 13.1 g/dL over 5 months (March to August 2024). May represent anemia of chronic disease.
